script:
Chapter 1 



Eric Wolf, my longtime crush, got hit with a heat herb the same day his father did.



Without a second of hesitation, I stripped down and walked straight into his father's room.



In my last life, I'd volunteered to be Eric's cure, and I ended up carrying his pups.



He hadn't shown an ounce of gratitude. Instead, he locked me in the cellar and tortured me every single day.



He was convinced I'd dosed him on purpose just to get into his bed.



His first love, Jenna, walked out of the pack over the whole mess, and the Rogues eventually tore her to pieces.



In the third year of our bond, he dragged me and our two pups out to the borderlands, his eyes burning with pure hatred.



"Jenna died right here!



"If you hadn't set me up, you scheming bitch, Jenna never would've left me!



"Everything she went through, you're going to suffer it ten times over!"



Then he tore our pups apart with his claws, right in front of me.



"No!"



A raw scream tore out of me, and a second later, his fangs were buried in my throat, snapping my neck clean through.



When my eyes opened again, I was back at the start, the day both Eric and his father had been drugged...



"Layla, you've got to do something! Eric can't hold on much longer!"



The maid, Martha, grabbed my arm in an iron grip.



Cold sweat broke out across my skin in an instant.



My neck still throbbed with the phantom feel of his fangs ripping through it.



The sight of my pups being torn apart alive kept playing on a loop behind my eyes.



"Eric got slipped a heat herb! You've always been crazy about him, haven't you? Get in there and help him!"



Martha's grip jerked me back to the moment.



I twisted out of her hold on instinct and took a step back.



My eyes dropped to my smooth arm. None of the scars from Eric's silver knife were there yet.



I... Had I really been reborn?



Chapter 2



And I'd come back to the perfect moment, the one where every tragedy could still be undone...



"Layla, what are you standing around for? Get in there and help him!"



My answer came out flat and cold.



"I'm not going. Go get Jenna. She's the one Eric actually loves."



Martha just stared at me, lost, so I forced myself to look like I was worried about Eric.



"Hurry, Martha! If you keep dragging your feet, Eric's not going to make it!"



The second Martha hurried off to find Jenna, I let out a long breath.



In my last life, I'd been so worried about Eric that I pushed that door open myself.



He'd hauled me onto the bed and driven himself into me, rough and merciless, his hard cock filling me to the hilt.



I'd been crushing on Eric for years. Being his cure was something I'd actually wanted.



So even though it hurt, I'd choked back every cry, held him tighter instead of pushing him off, and matched his every brutal thrust to help him ride out the heat...



Jenna had shown up too late. She stood outside the door, watching me through the crack the entire night.



I was such an idiot back then. I thought she was worried about me.



The next morning, Jenna packed up and left the pack, telling everyone Eric had betrayed her.



Then I turned up pregnant, and Eric's father, the pack Alpha, came down on him hard until he had no choice but to bond with me.



Getting bonded to Eric after pining over him for years should've been the happiest moment of my life.



Instead, the bond brought three years of pure torture.



Chapter 3



"If you hadn't drugged me on purpose, I never would've bonded with someone like you! Jenna left because of you!"



It didn't matter how I tried to defend myself. He'd decided I was nothing but a schemer who'd trapped him, and nothing was going to change his mind.



After that, he wouldn't so much as look at me. He'd sneer that the pup in my belly probably belonged to some stray wolf and I was just pinning it on him.



Then word came that the Rogues had tortured Jenna to death.



He went berserk and dragged me and the pups out to the borderlands.



Right in front of me, he tore the pups apart, then lunged and snapped my neck in his jaws.



The memory of my dead pups and the ice in his eyes made me want to drive a knife through him right there.



But death would be too easy. I owed my pups their vengeance, and I was going to make him pay ten times over.



I forced the hatred back down, unbuttoned my dress, and walked toward the other room.



Kane Wolf, Eric's Alpha father, had been hit with the same herb today.



Kane's quarters always had guards posted at the door.



But every one of them had been pulled away to deal with Eric, which left the path wide open.



Everyone in the pack knew Kane had loved his mate beyond reason.



After she died in childbirth, no woman had been able to get close to him.



Pack gossip even said Kane's heart had died with her, that he'd never take another partner in this life.



That was the only reason Eric had ended up the pack's sole heir.



But what if... what if Kane could have another pup this time?



Chapter 4



I paused and glanced back at Eric's door.



The door hung half open. Inside, Jenna's soft moans tangled with Eric's strangled growls and drifted out into the hallway.



I didn't care. I turned and pushed open Kane's door.



The water in the bathroom was cut off.



Kane was propped against the wall, soaked through, his black shirt plastered to every ridge of his abs. He looked like he was barely holding on.



At the sound of the door, his head whipped up, eyes instantly wary.



The moment our eyes locked, I caught the hunger burning in his.



His throat worked once. A heartbeat later, he yanked me hard against his chest.



His mouth came down on mine, scorching and relentless.    



By noon the next day, I walked out of the room wrapped in my torn dress.



Before I left, Kane had leaned in and left a temporary mark at the back of my neck.



His tone was dead serious. "The Rogues are stirring up trouble at the border. I'm going to deal with it. When I get back, I'm announcing you as the new Luna to the whole pack."



He gave me one last look and walked out with the rest of his Betas.



I'd barely separated from Kane before I ran into Jenna in the hallway.



She was carrying a bowl of broth, heading straight for Eric's room.



The moment she saw me, she stopped, raked her eyes over me head to toe, and lifted her chin in pure arrogance.



"Going somewhere in a rush, Layla?"



She planted herself square in my path, her face twisted with contempt.



"Look at the state of you... If you couldn't get your hooks into Eric, did you go run off to those filthy Rogues instead? You've got no shame at all."



Chapter 5



I lifted my eyes and looked her dead in the face. "Are you talking about yourself?"



Jenna scoffed in fury. "I'm nothing like you. I'm going to bond with Eric. You're just some easy slut anyone can have a turn with."



In my last life, Jenna had stood outside that door watching the entire night, and I'd believed she was worried about me.



After all, the two of us had been close enough that the whole pack knew about it.



It wasn't until she left the pack that I figured out she'd been trashing me to Eric the whole time.



That was why it had been so easy for Eric to believe I was nothing but a schemer who'd drugged him.



This time around, she still had that same ugly look on her face.



But none of it mattered now.



If I'd carried Eric's pups last time, I'd carry Kane's this time around without a doubt.



When Kane came back, I'd take Luna's seat for myself and destroy this lying pair with my own hands.



I glanced at the bowl of broth in her hands. "You'd better hurry up and go wait on your precious Eric."



I was about to walk past her when footsteps came up behind me.



Eric strolled over, slipped an arm around Jenna's waist, and buried his face in her neck, nuzzling against her.



"Baby, you must be exhausted from last night. Just ignore her. Come back to bed with me for a bit."



Jenna clung to his arm with a pout. "I can't. I've still got work at the pack workshop."



Then she shot me another scornful look.



"Layla here seems to have all the time in the world. Must be nice, earning a living on your back."



Eric's brow knitted together. "What does she matter? Baby, you belong to me now. You don't have to do that crap work anymore."



He turned to me. "So you sent your maid to fetch Jenna for me last night? At least you've got some sense of your own place. But you'd better stay in it and quit fantasizing about anything you can't have."



In my last life, Eric had shown up to save me when I was being bullied.



That was how I'd fallen for him, and the whole pack had known about my feelings.



Chapter 6



After I got pregnant, I'd asked him once whether he was actually willing to bond with me.



He'd nodded without missing a beat. "Of course. You just focus on taking good care of our pups."



Then his father handed down the Alpha title, and that was when his real face came out.



"If you hadn't planned it all out and drugged me, Jenna never would've left!



"And whatever's in your belly, who knows if it's even mine?"



Eric had been a liar from start to finish.



I gave him an indifferent nod. "Got it."



This time around, since Jenna had run in to save him, the two of them could stay together for all I cared.



I'd just see how long he got to look that pleased with himself.



I was about to walk off when a gust of wind pushed the collar of my coat open.



Eric's eyes locked onto my neck, where Kane had left a fresh red mark.



"Who put that there?" He grabbed my arm in a vice grip.



Jenna jumped in immediately, her voice all sugar-sweet.



"Baby, I saw her with my own eyes this morning. She was coming out of Shawn's quarters. I never expected her to go after people in your own circle just because she can't get to you."



Eric whipped his hand across my face in pure rage, and my cheek was swelling within seconds.



"You're that hard up for a man? You'd even take Shawn, that pack guard dog? I must've been blind to ever think you were anything but a slut."



I yanked my arm out of his grip and was about to tell him to go to hell.



Without warning, Jenna let out a sharp scream.



"It burns!"



Jenna was crouched on the floor, broth splashed everywhere. She was clutching her arm and sobbing about the pain.



Eric let go of me in an instant and rushed over to her.



Chapter 7



With werewolf healing, a little burn like that wouldn't even leave a trace, but he was still treating her like she was made of glass.



Jenna rested her chin on his shoulder, hooked her arm around his neck, and shot me a look soaked in triumph.



In my last life, I didn't hear about what happened to Jenna after she left the pack until much later.



After she walked out, she wandered alone along the edges of every pack she could find.



A lone omega out there with no pack protection was nothing but a walking target.



A group of Rogues had cornered her, and they'd torn her apart while she was still alive.



The day the news got back to the pack, Eric had gone insane. He'd dragged me out of the cellar and hauled me all the way to the borderlands.



"Jenna died right here! If you hadn't set me up, you scheming bitch, she never would've left the pack at all! Every bit of it, you're going to pay back ten times over!"



Then, right in front of me, he tore both my pups to pieces.



It was the exact way the Rogues had killed Jenna.



I walked back to my room alone and shut the door behind me.



I laid a hand against my stomach. A faint pulse fluttered under my palm, and I could feel the pup inside me with perfect clarity.



I stood in front of the mirror for a long time.



The pup growing in me was Kane's.



Once Kane came back and broke the news, how was the whole pack going to treat Eric?



And as for you, Eric, you wouldn't be the pack's sole heir anymore.  



After Kane left, I'd thought all I had to do was sit tight and wait for him to come back.



Chapter 8



Eric didn't give me that luxury.



The moment Kane was out the door, Eric called the whole pack together as acting Alpha.



He announced his relationship with Jenna and ordered every last pack member to take orders from her.



Word ripped through the pack within hours.



Everyone decided I'd thrown myself at Eric, gotten rejected, and turned around to sleep with Kane's guard, Shawn, out of spite.



On top of that, they all set out to suck up to Jenna by piling on me twice as hard.



At the daily meat distribution, I got nothing but the rancid scraps. People knocked over my water bucket on purpose. Someone tore a gaping hole in my roof, and I couldn't find a single dry spot to sleep on at night.



The women hurled insults at me right to my face.



"What a disgrace. She'll even spread her legs for a guard dog."



"No mother, no father, born a worthless mutt. Filth runs in the blood."



Jenna stood at my door with her arms crossed and let out a fake little sigh.



"Layla, just go apologize to Eric. He's got a soft heart. He'll forgive you for sure."



I didn't bother answering and kept working on what was in my hands.



That was how these people operated. They flocked to whoever held the power.



None of it touched me.



All I had to do was wait.



The second Kane was back, every bit of this was going to flip.



When that day came, every debt they owed me was going to be paid back ten times over.



*****



The next day, the moment I stepped out of my hut, Eric pinned me into a corner. His hand clamped around my throat and slammed me against the stone wall.



"You really can't stand being alone, can you? Cut things off with Shawn and come back to my side, and I'll let you stay with me the way you did in our last life."



He'd been reborn too.



He remembered all of it. He remembered how he'd tortured me, how he'd slaughtered our pups, how he'd snapped my neck between his teeth.



He remembered every second of it, and he still had the gall to talk about 'coming back to his side.'



My breath was being crushed out of me, but I held his stare.



"I'd sooner die than let trash like you near me again!"



"You want to die? You're nothing but a woman who's been touched by some low-life male wolf. Who else would want you? Quit acting like you've got any pride left!"



Chapter 9



I twisted my head away from his grip and looked at him with open disgust.



"Eric, you make my skin crawl."



Pure fury took him over, and he grabbed my head and smashed it into the stone wall again and again.



"If you don't know what's good for you, then get the hell out of this pack!"



Blood streamed down the side of my head, but I shoved past the pain, pushed him off me, and bolted.



I'd only made it a few steps when something cut me short.



Jenna came tearing out from the side and threw herself in my path, reaching for me.



"Layla, don't go!"



I twisted out of the way and didn't waste a glance on her.



Right at that second, Jenna let out a piercing scream and threw herself to the ground with the whole pack watching.



The whole crowd went dead silent.



Eric tore over and scooped her up. Blood was running from Jenna's forehead and her face had gone bone-white.



"Get the healer!"



Eric roared at the crowd and then whipped his glare onto me.



"Layla! You pushed her!"



Two Gammas came at me on the spot and threw me down hard, forcing me onto my knees in front of him.



My knees cracked against the stone, and the blood started pouring.



"I didn't push her."



I braced my hands on the ground and pushed myself back up, fixing Eric with a cold stare.



"Every single person here saw it. She threw herself down."



"You've still got the nerve to talk back?"



Eric drove his boot into my shoulder and slammed me back to the ground.



He yanked the dagger off his belt, fisted a handful of my hair, and forced my head up so I'd look him in the eye.



"Let's see how long that mouth of yours can hold out today."



Every pack member around us was staring at the dirt. Not one of them dared make a sound.



Before the healer carried Jenna off, she shot me one last taunting smirk.



The cold edge of the blade pressed into my left shoulder, and the skin started to weep blood.



"Drop to your knees and beg Jenna's forgiveness, and I'll let you live."



Chapter 10（最后免费章）



I locked my eyes on his and refused to look away.



"I did nothing wrong! Eric, you're going to regret this."



That broke whatever was left of his control. He raised the dagger high and drove it straight into my left shoulder.



The pain stole my voice, but he wasn't done. He kept twisting the blade inside the wound, around and around.



He'd done exactly this to me in my last life.



He knew it was the only way to burn through werewolf healing fast enough. Every time he'd done it, I'd screamed and begged him to stop.



This time, when I refused to beg, he pulled the dagger free and shot one of his men a look.



His man caught the meaning instantly and handed over a silver blade.



He was dead set on killing me this time. Was I really going to die the same brutal death all over again?



Eric took the silver dagger. "Last chance. On your knees. Apologize."



I stared at the silver blade in his hand. I knew the second it went in, the wound in my shoulder would never heal.



But I didn't kneel.



I pulled my back straight and locked my eyes on his. "I did nothing wrong!"



If the crime wasn't mine, I was never going to claim it.



"You've got a death wish. You brought this on yourself!"



A heartbeat later, he raised the silver blade and drove it down at me with everything he had.



A thunderous crack split the air.



It rolled out alongside a wave of crushing Alpha power, and Eric was kicked clean across the clearing, sailing ten feet through the air.



"You bastard! Don't you touch my future Luna! I'll send you straight to hell!"



My eyes flew up.



Kane was here. He'd actually come back!  



Chapter 11



Eric slammed into the ground and spat out a mouthful of blood.



Dead silence fell over the entire courtyard.



Kane's Alpha pressure forced every pack member to their knees, and not one of them dared lift their head.



Kane didn't spare anyone else a glance. He strode straight to me and steadied my shoulders with both hands.



"Does it hurt?"



"No."



I looked up at him, and the weight that had sat on my chest for days finally lifted.



Kane shrugged off his coat and wrapped it around me, then turned to face Eric where he lay on the ground.



"Who gave you the nerve to touch what's mine?"



Eric pushed himself up off the dirt and wiped the blood from the corner of his mouth.



"Dad! She's playing you! She's been screwing around with Shawn, and she shoved Jenna down on purpose!"



"Shut up. Shawn has been with me this whole time. There's no way he's been touching Layla."



Eric's face drained of color in a heartbeat.



Jenna was lying in the healer's arms. She scrambled upright and pressed a hand to her forehead.



"Alpha Kane, I tripped on my own. None of this had anything to do with Layla. It's my fault for running my mouth and setting Eric off."



She put on her best pitiful act, but Kane didn't waste so much as a glance on her.



"Didn't I tell you before I left to stay in your room and keep out of trouble?



"Starting now, you don't step outside that door without my permission."



"Dad!"



Eric's head snapped up.



A stunned hush dropped over the crowd.



Chapter 12



Every trace of smugness drained off Eric's face, and he let out a snarl of pure disbelief.



"What did you just say?!"



Kane didn't even glance at Eric.



He bent down, scooped me into his arms, and started toward the main quarters.



As we passed through the kneeling crowd, he stopped in his tracks.



"From today on, Layla is your future Luna. Cross her again and you're crossing me. You all know what that means."



The entire crowd answered as one. "Yes, Alpha Kane!"



I leaned into Kane's chest and listened to the steady beat of his heart.



I caught one last look at Eric's and Jenna's faces and felt absolutely nothing.



This was only the beginning.



Every debt the two of you ran up in our last life, I was going to collect on, piece by piece.



Kane carried me back to the main quarters and sent for the pack's best healer.



The healer finished his exam and said, "The wound itself isn't serious. The silver just sank into her system, so a few days of rest will set her right."



Kane gave a nod and dismissed him. He sat at the edge of the bed, eyes fixed on the bandages around my shoulder, his brow drawn into a tight knot. "I'm sorry. This was my fault. I never should've left you here on your own."



I shook my head. "It isn't on you. Eric crossed every line."



"Until you're fully healed, you won't have to lay eyes on him again."



I looked up at him with a tangle of feelings I couldn't sort through.



In my last life, no one had ever stood between me and harm like this.



Chapter 13



Eric had only ever hit me, screamed in my face, and locked me away in the cellar where daylight never reached.



Kane was someone I'd only ever planned to use, and yet, he made me feel safer than anyone ever had.



"Why did you come back early?" I asked.



"I was halfway there when I couldn't shake the feeling that something was wrong, so I turned my men around and rode back. Lucky I got here in time."



"Thank you."



Kane reached out and brushed his fingers against my uninjured hand. "Don't thank me. You're mine. Protecting you is the bare minimum."



*****



Over the next few days, Kane never left my side.



He changed my bandages on schedule, brought every meal to me himself, and slept in the room right next door. The second I made any sound at all, he was at my door.



He didn't say much, but every action he took carried real weight.



I could tell what he felt for me had gone beyond duty, but I didn't dare let myself dwell on it.



I'd gone after him for revenge in the first place, and I couldn't afford to let real feelings creep in.



That afternoon, I was sitting by the window, soaking up the sun, when I caught the sound of a few women murmuring outside.



"Do you really think Layla can be Luna? She's just an orphan with no father, no mother, not even a decent family to her name."



"Who knows? She probably pulled some cheap seduction trick to get her claws into Alpha Kane. Once the novelty wears off, he'll toss her aside for sure."



"Exactly. Jenna's the one who belongs with Eric. Once Eric inherits the Alpha title, Jenna's going to be Luna anyway."



A cold laugh slipped out under my breath.



Of course Jenna wasn't going to let things drop that easily.



Chapter 14



She didn't have the guts to come at me directly, so she'd taken to spreading rumors behind my back.



It didn't take long for Jenna to show up at my door. She was carrying a bowl of chicken broth, a fake smile plastered across her face.



"Layla, I made you some chicken broth to help you get your strength back. Don't take anything those women said to heart. They're just gossip."



I didn't reach for the bowl. I just stared her down. "You put them up to it, didn't you?"



Jenna's smile froze for a beat, and then she let out a long sigh.



"I really am looking out for you. Think about it. So many people in this pack don't accept you. Even if you make Luna, the life waiting for you afterward won't be easy."



"Here's a thought." She leaned in closer and dropped her voice to a whisper.



"You go tell Alpha Kane yourself that you don't want to be Luna anymore. I'll go to Eric and have him beg Alpha Kane to let you stay in the pack."



"How generous of you. So, how about you give up Eric yourself and let me be his wife?"



"What are you talking about?! Eric and I are truly in love!"



"So are Kane and I."



I leaned back in my chair and held her gaze.



"Maybe you should mind your own business. Spread one more rumor about me, and I'll have Kane throw you out of the pack."



Jenna ground her teeth together, shot me a vicious glare, and stormed out.



Chapter 15



She'd barely been gone a few minutes before Kane walked in.



"I heard all of it."



"Mm." I gave a small nod.



Kane's expression darkened. "I'll have someone deal with the women running their mouths. The next time Jenna shows up, tell the guards to throw her out."



"Okay."



Kane crossed over to my side and looked out the window, watching the path Jenna had taken.



"I'm not letting anyone hurt you. Not a single soul."  



Another three days passed, and the healer came back to check on me.



He examined the wound on my shoulder first and said it was healing well. The stitches could come out in another two days.



Then he laid his fingers across my pulse and reached out with his wolf-sense.



"How's the pup?"



Kane's voice came out rougher than usual, pitched lower than I'd ever heard it.



"The pup's holding strong!"



The healer spoke up quickly.



"Layla's constitution is excellent, and the pup is perfectly healthy. She just needs to avoid overworking herself and stay clear of any major stress."



"Good. Write up every precaution she needs to take throughout the pregnancy. The more detailed, the better. Have it on my desk by morning."



"Yes. I'll get started on it right away."



Once the healer was gone, it was just Kane and me in the room.



Kane crossed the floor and crouched down beside me until his eyes were level with my belly.



He didn't dare touch me. He only looked at the spot through the fabric of my dress with the softest gaze.



Chapter 16



A long stretch of silence passed before he lifted his head and looked at me.



His eyes were bright, and they held a tenderness I'd never seen in him before.



"Layla, thank you."



Something in my chest stirred when I looked back at him.



"This pup is yours, too."



"Mm."



Kane gave a small nod and folded his hand gently around mine.



"From here on out, I'm going to protect the two of you. I won't let anyone hurt either one of you."



That afternoon, Kane started arranging everything.



He assigned two of the most attentive Beta handmaids to take care of me, and he brought in the pack's best cook to prepare every meal according to the healer's diet plan.



He had every sharp object cleared out of the main quarters, and he ordered the floors relaid from scratch so I wouldn't slip.



He himself wouldn't leave my side for a second.



When the pack business needed handling, he brought the paperwork into my room and signed off on it from there.



When I slept, he sat in the chair next to the bed and watched over me.



When I woke, he poured me water and brought me fruit by hand.



The gossiping women from before came running to the main quarters that very night, dropped to their knees at the door, and begged for forgiveness.



Every one of them kept her head bowed too low to meet my eyes, and they couldn't stop choking out apologies.



I stood on the second-floor balcony and stared down at the dark mass of them without feeling a thing.



Chapter 17



In my last life, every single one of those women had watched Eric haul me out to the borderlands. They'd watched my pups get torn apart, and not one of them had spoken up.



Coming to beg my forgiveness now was a few lifetimes too late.



Kane came up beside me and slid his arm around my shoulders.



"How do you want to deal with them?"



"Make each of them chop ten bundles of firewood and haul them out to the border outpost. After that, cut them off from the fresh meat ration for a whole month. One more slip out of any of them, and they're out of the pack."



"Done." Kane gave a nod and turned to address the crowd below.



"You all heard her. Move."



"Thank you, Layla! Thank you, Alpha Kane!"



Everyone of them breathed out in relief. They knocked their foreheads against the dirt in thanks, then scrambled off in disgrace.



From across the grounds, I could hear Eric smashing things to pieces in his room. His roars carried all the way to where we stood.



"It's not possible! There's no way she's carrying my father's pup! I'm the heir! I am!"



The word that I was truly pregnant reached Jenna, too. She shut herself in her room and didn't come out for the entire day.



She wasn't the type to swallow defeat that easily, and I knew it.



She'd dig up something even uglier to come at me and my pup with.



Even so, I wasn't afraid.



I had Kane now, and I had the pup growing inside me.



I wasn't the same Layla from my last life anymore, the one who'd faced everything alone.



This time around, I was going to win.  



Chapter 18



The next few days passed more quietly than the pack had been in weeks.



Eric stayed confined to his room and never came out to stir up trouble again.



Jenna kept her head down, too. She stayed in her room and barely showed her face.



But I knew it for what it was. This was the calm before the storm.



Jenna wasn't the kind to walk away quietly. She'd come at me with something even nastier soon enough.



I asked Kane to put eyes on Jenna and track every move she made.



Just as I'd guessed, a few days later, the Beta tailing her came in with a report.



"Alpha Kane, Layla, Jenna snuck out of the pack last night and went west, into Blackwood Forest."



"What was she doing out there?" Kane asked, his brow tightening.



"We tracked her to the edge of Blackwood but didn't dare follow her in. We saw her leave a cloth bundle at the base of one of the big trees."



My stomach sank. "What was inside?"



"No idea. We didn't risk going in to grab it. The Rogues would've been on us."



"Don't bother with that. Whatever's in that bundle is our defense layout and food supplies. She's feeding the Rogues, and she wants them to come down on the pack."



Kane's expression turned grim in an instant.



"How does she have the nerve to side with the Rogues? That's a death sentence for betraying the entire pack."



"There's nothing she wouldn't dare at this point. She's backed into a corner with no way out, and she'd rather throw in with the Rogues than let me come out on top."



"What do you want to do?"



Chapter 19



"It's simple." I looked Kane straight in the eye.



"If she wants the Rogues to come, we play dumb and let the Luna ceremony go ahead exactly as planned. The Rogues will show up, and we'll have an ambush ready to take every last one of them."



Kane gave a nod. "All right. We do it your way."



That same night, Kane called every warrior in the pack to a closed-door meeting.



He reworked the pack's entire defense layout from the ground up and set three rings of ambushers around the ceremony grounds.



He sent a unit of elite warriors to lock down the exit out of Blackwood Forest and cut off any chance the Rogues had of slipping away.



Once everything was in place, all we had to do was wait for Jenna and the Rogues to walk into the trap.



The Luna ceremony was set for three days out.



The morning of the ceremony, the moment first light broke, Jenna snuck out of the pack again and made her way back to Blackwood Forest.



The Beta who'd been tailing her came back with another report.



"Jenna met with one of the Rogue ringleaders. She told them to kill Layla first, then go for Alpha Kane."



"Good," Kane said with a nod. "Pass the word to every warrior. We move on her signal."



Around noon, the ceremony got underway.



The whole pack had gathered in the square.



I stood at Kane's side in a long white gown.



Kane took my hand in his and was about to name me the new Luna.



Then a wave of war cries tore through the air from somewhere down the slope.



Chapter 20



A pack of Rogues came pouring through the rear gate with weapons drawn and barreled straight for the square.



The crowd dissolved into chaos in a heartbeat.



Jenna stood in the middle of it all, a smug little smile playing across her face.



She thought her plan had landed.



What she didn't know was that we'd been waiting for them the entire time.



The moment the Rogues hit the square, Kane gave the order.



The warriors hidden around the perimeter charged out of cover. The clash of war cries thundered across the grounds.



The Rogues had no time to react and got driven back foot by foot.



They were nothing more than scattered exiles each pack had thrown out, with no discipline and no coordination. They were never a match for trained warriors.



Within half an hour, most of the Rogues lay dead in pools of blood. The handful who tried to scramble back toward Blackwood Forest got cut off by the warriors guarding the exit and hauled back in.



We took seven of them alive, the ringleader of the attack included, the one with a long scar slashed across his face.



Jenna had been standing right at the front of the crowd, that triumphant smirk still on her face as she waited to watch the Rogues tear me apart.



The instant she saw her Rogues wiped out to the last man, her smile froze and shattered.



While every eye was elsewhere, she took two slow steps back and tried to slip away behind the main quarters.



"Take her."



Kane's voice carried no rise or fall, and he didn't even turn his head to look at her.



Two Gammas standing nearby lunged at her, one on each side, and pinned her face down in the dirt.



Chapter 21



Her face hit the mud hard, her hair fell across her eyes in a tangled mess, and every shred of her composure was gone.



Jenna thrashed against their grip and screamed. "Let me go! What gives you the right to lay a hand on me? I haven't done a thing!"



"For siding with the Rogues, for trying to bring down the whole pack, for plotting to kill Alpha Kane and me. That's what gives us the right."



I walked over and stood above her, looking down. The sunlight caught me square on, and the shadow it threw fell right across her face.



"I didn't do any of that! You're lying through your teeth! They broke in on their own. I had nothing to do with any of it!"



Jenna lifted her head, eyes bloodshot, and called out to the pack members around her.



"Don't any of you believe her! She's framing me! She's just jealous that Eric loves me, not her!"



A cold laugh slipped out of me, and I turned to the Rogue ringleader being held off to the side.



"Tell everyone who handed your people the defense layout, who fed you the supplies and weapons, and who arranged to throw open the rear gate at noon today."



The scarred ringleader was shaking from head to toe, too rattled to lift his head. He thrust a finger straight at Jenna.



"Her! It was her! She met with me last night to lock in the timing for today!"



"You're making it up! I've never seen you before in my life!"



Jenna shrieked the words.



Chapter 22



"I'm not making it up! That silver chain around your neck? You left it at my place the last time we met!"



The scarred ringleader pulled a thin silver chain from inside his jacket and threw it down on the ground in front of Jenna.



"Your name's engraved right on the back of it!"



The crowd erupted on the spot.



"So it really was her! She's the one who sold us out to the Rogues!"



"What a piece of work! She didn't care if everyone of us died!"



"Kill her! Kill the traitor!"



A few pack members with family wounded in the fight were already snatching stones off the ground and hurling them at Jenna.



Jenna sank down into the dirt, staring at the silver chain, with nothing left to say in her defense.



Every piece of evidence sat right there in plain sight, and she couldn't talk her way out of any of it.



Right then, Eric came tearing through the crowd. He wrenched himself free from the two warriors guarding him, threw himself in front of Jenna, and locked her behind his back.



He yanked a freshly sharpened dagger out from inside his coat and held it across his own chest, his eyes burning red enough to bleed as he faced down the warriors closing in on him.



"Anyone lays a finger on her, and I gut them where they stand!"



Everyone froze.



No one had expected Eric to lose his mind this completely. He'd actually drawn a blade on every warrior in the pack to shield a woman who'd betrayed all of them.



"Dad! You can't kill her!" Eric shouted. "She's being framed! Layla set the whole thing up! She's the one who manufactured the evidence to take Jenna down!"



"Eric, you've made enough of a scene."



Kane's face went cold, and his eyes held nothing in them.



Chapter 23



"The witnesses are here. The evidence is here. The entire pack is watching. And you're still standing in her way?"



"Jenna is the only woman I've ever loved in this life! If you want her dead, you're going to have to come through me first! Don't doubt for a second that I mean it!" Eric shouted.



I looked at him without feeling a thing.



It didn't matter how many times we got to do this over. Eric was never going to be anyone but this.  



"The only woman you've ever loved?"



Kane let out a low scoff and took two slow steps forward.



His presence pressed down on the entire square, and the air seemed to freeze with every step he took.



Eric flinched back on instinct. The hand gripping the dagger started shaking.



"Is that what you told her in your last life, too?"



The words came out soft, but they hit Eric like a thunderclap going off right next to his ear.



The dagger slipped from his fingers and clattered to the ground.



He lifted his head and stared at Kane in pure disbelief. His lips trembled, but no sound came out for the longest time.



Every trace of rage and arrogance drained out of his face in an instant. Pure terror was all that remained.



"You... how could you..."



"How could I know?" Kane cut him off, his eyes cold as ice.



"I also know that in your last life, you locked Layla in the abandoned cellar behind the main quarters and beat her for three years straight."



Eric collapsed flat to the ground.



His eyes locked onto Kane, hollowed out with horror and despair.



He'd never dreamed anyone would uncover his secret about being reborn.



Even less had he dreamed that the person to do it would be his own father.



Chapter 24	



The pack members below the platform were lost. None of them could make sense of what Kane was saying. All they could see was Eric, who'd been swaggering and shouting threats moments ago, suddenly crumple to the dirt like every bone in his body had been pulled out.



Only I felt my heart slam against my ribs hard enough to break out of my chest.



I stared at Kane's back, my mind a tangled mess.



How could he possibly know any of this?



These were things that had happened in my last life, the nightmares I'd buried deepest in my chest.



Apart from Eric and me, no third person should have known. Unless...



"You thought a second chance was going to change anything?"



Kane stared down at where Eric lay on the ground and went on.



"You thought sending Jenna to the Rogues ahead of time would let you catch me off guard, take me and Layla out, and slide right into the Alpha title? You were a fool. From the first day you came back, I've known every move you've made."



Eric lay sprawled in the dirt, trembling without pause.



He had nothing left to say in his own defense.



Every layer of the act he'd built, every calculation he'd run, broke apart to nothing in that single moment.



He'd assumed he was the only one carrying memories back into this life. He'd assumed every piece would fall under his hand. The truth was that from the very start, he'd only ever been a pathetic clown.



Jenna was just as stunned.



She looked at Eric sprawled on the ground, then at Kane's blank face, and finally understood she'd been played from the start to the end.



She lunged at Eric, grabbed fistfuls of his hair, and went at him with her nails and teeth. "You lied to me! You actually lied to me! You told me you'd make me Luna! You told me you'd love me forever! You filthy liar!"



Eric let her tear into him without lifting a hand.



Chapter 25



Kane gave a sharp gesture.



Two warriors stepped in and forced the two of them apart.



"Jenna sided with an outside enemy, plotted to bring down this pack, and meant to kill its own. Her crimes are as black as they come. Tomorrow at noon, she hangs in the square. Her body will be left on display for one full day as a warning to anyone who thinks to follow."



Kane's voice carried clear across the square, with no room left in it for argument.



"As for Eric, you aided a traitor, withheld knowledge of treason, and repeatedly tried to harm the future Luna. Because you carry my blood, I'll spare your life. The sentence is life in Blackstone Hold at the border, with no contact with any living soul, and no setting foot beyond your cell. Any attempt to defy the order is met with death."



The sentence cut deeper than killing Eric outright.



Blackstone Hold was the most feared place the pack had. It sat tens of meters below the earth, carved into solid rock, dark and damp and cut off from sunlight all year round.



Each day brought a single ration of stale black bread and a swallow of cold water.



He'd spend the rest of his life alone down there, in a place daylight never reached.



Eric raised his head and stared at Kane. His eyes were drowned in despair. But he didn't say a word.



He knew he'd lost every right to beg for mercy.



Not a soul felt sorry for them. They'd brought every bit of it on themselves.



I stood where I was, eyes fixed on Kane's back, the questions in my head growing heavier by the second.



Just how much else did he know?  



Chapter 26



At noon the next day, Jenna hung from the gallows in the square.



Not a single voice rose to plead for her.



Every pack member stood below and watched until the last breath left her body, then went their separate ways.



That same afternoon, two warriors marched Eric out to Blackstone Hold at the border.



I stood at the door of the main quarters and watched his back vanish into the far end of the grasslands. He didn't turn around, and he didn't call out again.



In our last life, he'd shut me in that cellar and tortured me for three years. In this one, I'd locked him into a darker pit for the rest of his.



It evened the scales.



The day after Jenna and Eric were dealt with, the pack held the Luna ceremony.



The square was packed with every member of the pack, all of them in their cleanest clothes, every face turned solemn with respect.



Kane stood up on the high platform with the silver Moon Goddess Pendant cradled in his hands.



That pendant was the symbol every Luna in the pack had worn for the past century, etched with the pack's totem.



He crossed the platform to me with a soft look in his eyes.



"Layla, will you take your place as Luna of this pack and stand by my side to protect it?"



"I will."



I held his gaze and said it like I meant it.



Kane bent his head and fastened the cool chain around my neck.



The metal lay against my skin with a faint chill, and even so, it settled something deep in me.



Every pack member below dropped to one knee and laid a right hand over the left side of the chest.



I looked out over the cheering pack and then turned to find Kane beside me.



He was already watching me, a small smile in his eyes.



The sunlight poured down over both of us, warm and bright. Every dark, painful thing in my past felt like it had finally let me go.



Chapter 27



Once the ceremony was wrapped, Kane took my hand and led me back to the main quarters.



He wrapped his arms around me from behind and rested his chin on my shoulder, his voice low and tender.



"It's over. Nobody is ever going to lay a hand on you again."



I gave a soft hum and leaned back into him.



But the question I'd been carrying never went away.



How was it that Kane knew about our last life?



I'd asked him a few times. He only ever stroked the top of my head and answered with a smile.



"I'll tell you down the road. For now, you just focus on taking care of yourself."



The days slipped by, one after another.



My belly kept growing, and I could feel the pup kicking now, clear as anything.



Kane handed every pack matter off to the elders and stayed at my side day in and day out.



He never once said the words out loud, but every single thing he did told me he cared.



And somehow, he always knew exactly what I liked and what I couldn't stand.



Those were details from my last life. In this one, I'd never said a word about any of it to anyone.



The more time passed, the more sure I became that Kane was hiding something from me.



Every time I worked up the nerve to ask, one look at the softness in his eyes pulled the words right back into my throat.



I was scared to learn the answer, and just as scared to live without one.



Chapter 28



That afternoon, Kane went out on a patrol around the pack borders and wouldn't be back until evening.



Stuck alone in the main quarters with nothing to do, I decided to tidy up his study.



His study was always in chaos, books and papers thrown around every surface.



I climbed onto a stool to pull down a stack of books from the very top of the shelf.



My arm was at a bad angle, and I knocked a dark wooden box right off the back of the shelf.



The box hit the floor with a sharp crack and burst open.



Whatever had been inside spilled out across the floor.



I crouched down to pick it all up, and the second my eyes landed on what was there, my whole body locked in place.



It was a battered gray wool scarf.



I'd knit it myself in our last life, back when I'd first arrived at the pack, with Eric in mind.



Back then, I'd believed that as long as I was good to him, he might actually start to see me.



I'd stayed up three nights in a row finishing that scarf and given it to him on his birthday.



He hadn't spared it a second glance before he'd tossed it into the trash can next to him. I'd always assumed the scarf had been burned years ago.



I'd never imagined Kane had been the one to hold onto it. And he'd kept it safe through every one of those years.



Inside the box, there was also a thick leather-bound journal.



Its cover had yellowed with time, the corners worn down, soft, and it looked like it had been opened more times than I could count.



My heart was racing, and my hands wouldn't stop shaking.



Chapter 29



I hesitated for a long time, but in the end, I reached out and turned back the first page of the journal.  



The first page of the journal opened on a single date.



It was the day I'd died in my last life.



The handwriting was rushed, as if it had been put down under the pressure of grief sharp enough to break someone.



"Layla died today. Eric waited until I was away from the pack, then dragged Layla out to the borderlands and left her there for the Rogues to tear apart.



"I held her cold body through the whole night.



"Only then did it hit me. I'd been in love with her for a long time without ever admitting it.



"I was the Alpha of this pack. I was the strongest warrior in it.



"I'd told myself I had no right to fall for the mate of my own son.



"I let the title and the rules tie my hands. I watched her suffer. I watched Eric break her down piece by piece, and I did nothing.



"Could a man this weak ever really lead a pack?



"I buried her at the edge of Blackwood Forest, in the field of sunflowers there.



"Sunflowers had been her favorite in that life. She used to say sunflowers always turned their faces toward the sun, the way hope always finds a way to keep going.



"I built a small wooden cabin in front of her grave.



Chapter 30



"I lived in that cabin for three years.



"Every single day, I brought her a fresh bunch of sunflowers and sat beside her and talked.



"I told myself, if there was ever another life waiting for me, I'd never agree to be an Alpha again.



"I'd just want to be the kind of man who could keep her safe.



"I'd find her early this time. I'd tell her I loved her, and I'd never let her take another moment of pain.



"If only there really were another life waiting."



I read the words on the page as tear after tear fell onto the paper and bled the ink into pale blurs.



Someone had been watching over me in secret all along, his heart aching for me the whole way.



I hadn't been fighting through any of this on my own.



He'd already fallen in love with me in our last life.



I kept turning the pages. Everything after that had been written after his rebirth.



"I came back. I woke up on that very night.



"When I saw Layla push open my door and walk in, I almost broke down right there.



"The Moon Goddess had heard my prayer.



"She'd given me one chance to make it right.



"This time around, I will never let her go again."



I clutched the journal to my chest, sank to the floor on my knees, and cried until I had no breath left.



Every coincidence I'd brushed off had been arranged by him with care.



Every kindness I'd taken for granted had been him giving everything in silence.



Behind me, the door opened.



Chapter 31



Kane was back.



He stepped into the study, took in the sight of me on the floor and the scarf and journal scattered around me, and froze in place.



He stood where he was and watched me, a flicker of nerves and uncertainty in his eyes.



Neither of us said a word.



After a long stretch of silence, he crossed to me slowly, crouched down in front of me, and brushed the tears off my cheeks with a gentle hand.



"I'm sorry." His voice came out rough. "I never wanted to keep this from you. I was just... afraid you might not forgive me."



I threw myself into his arms and held on tight.



I sobbed into his chest. "There's nothing to forgive. Kane, thank you. Thank you for finding me. Thank you for never giving up on me."



Kane held me, his body trembling against mine. He buried his face in my hair, his voice catching in his throat.



"Silly girl, I'm the one who owes you the thanks. Thank you for coming back to me. Thank you for giving me a family."



Sunlight streamed in through the window and spilled over both of us.



From somewhere in the distance, children's laughter floated through the air.



I leaned into Kane's chest, ran my hand across the curve of my belly, and felt a happiness so full it left no room for anything else.



Every painful thing in our past had finally dissolved into nothing.



What was waiting for us was endless light and warmth, nothing less.



Kane and I, and the pup growing inside me, were going to live happily ever after, together, forever.
synopsis: 一位名为Layla的狼族女子在重生后，回到了决定她悲惨命运的起点。上一世，她为救暗恋对象Eric而自愿成为其热草的解药，却反被诬陷下药，遭受了长达三年的囚禁与折磨。最终，Eric当着她的面残忍杀害了他们共同的两个幼崽，并咬断了她的脖子。重生归来，Layla决心复仇，她放弃了Eric，转而选择进入其父亲、Alpha Kane的房间。Kane是位深情的鳏夫，在上一世便已暗恋Layla多年，并同样带着记忆重生。Layla怀上Kane的孩子后，Eric与他的真爱Jenna联手设计陷害她，甚至勾结流浪狼族企图灭口。然而，Kane早已洞悉一切，设下圈套将敌人一网打尽。最终，Jenna被处决，Eric被囚禁于永不见天日的黑石堡，而Layla则与Kane携手，成为狼族的新月神女，开启了一段被救赎与守护的崭新人生。
